text.title: Requirements Validation Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real applications of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Every requirement is verdict=pass with no annotations.
antipattern.root-cause: Reviewer does not engage with the content.
antipattern.fix: Sample-audit: pick 5 random requirements per cycle; require reviewer to defend the verdict.

antipattern.symptom: Requirements pass validation without engineering feasibility input.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skips the engineering review step.
antipattern.fix: Force a feasibility field with engineer's name + estimate.

antipattern.symptom: Verdict exists but reviewer field is blank.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats validation as a self-service form.
antipattern.fix: Require named reviewer + timestamp per verdict.
